reasoning: The informal statement claims ∫₁⁵ x·ln(x) dx = (25·ln 5 - 12)/2 (it says "indefinite integral" but the explicit bounds 1 to 5 make it clearly a definite integral — a harmless terminology slip). The Lean statement `∫ x in Set.Icc 1 5, x * log x = (25 * log 5 - 12) / 2` expresses exactly this: with `open Real`, `log` is the natural logarithm (ln), and the set integral over `Set.Icc 1 5` coincides with the definite Riemann/Lebesgue integral from 1 to 5 for the continuous integrand x·ln(x). The numerical value (25·ln 5 - 12)/2 is correct (F(x) = (x²/2)ln(x) - x²/4 gives F(5) - F(1) = (25/2)ln 5 - 6). The hypothesis structure, quantification, types, and conclusion all match.
